title: angelica archangelica subsp. litoralis
intro: Angelica archangelica subsp. litoralis, also known as sea angelica, is a subspecies of the angelica plant that is native to coastal areas and has adapted to grow in a saline environment. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of sea angelica include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of sea angelica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
